title: Spec: Add explicit undefined behavior section (or state there is none)
Support never type (\!) in function return types
description: Add support for the never type (\!) in function return type annotations. This would allow declaring functions that never return, such as 'fn diverge() -> \! { loop {} }'. This enables unreachable code detection after calls to never-returning functions. Currently the Never type exists internally but cannot be written in source code.
close_reason: Implemented support for ! (never) type in function return type annotations. Added parsing in type_parser, well-known symbol in interner, and resolve_type handling in sema. Note: while true {} still produces () type (not !); a future loop construct will be needed for functions that actually diverge.

title: AArch64 compilation always routes to macOS linker regardless of target OS
description:
The backend always routes to the macOS linker, even when targeting Aarch64Linux, so cross-compiling/running on Linux/ARM fails outright (wrong flags/-lSystem).

Location: lib.rs lines 368-394.

Fix: The backend should branch on options.target.os()—use the internal ELF linker (or Linux linker invocation) for Aarch64Linux and keep the macOS path only for Aarch64Macos.

Note: We may want to just error for now since we don't really support AArch64 Linux yet.
close_reason: Fixed: Added error for unsupported aarch64-linux target. The compile_aarch64 function now returns a clear error message directing users to use aarch64-macos or x86-64-linux instead.

title: Implement comptime and type-level computation (Phase 3)
description:
Implement compile-time evaluation with types as first-class values.

## ADR Reference
See docs/designs/adr-016-comptime.md

## Key Features
- comptime keyword for compile-time parameters and blocks
- Types as first-class values at compile time
- Comptime interpreter for evaluation
- Generic syntax sugar (fn foo<T>() desugars to comptime)
- Type intrinsics: size_of, align_of, has_field, etc.
- Specialization based on type properties

## Implementation Steps
1. Comptime interpreter core (ComptimeValue, basic evaluation)
2. Type as value representation
3. Comptime parameter parsing and tracking
4. Comptime block evaluation
5. Generic instantiation and caching
6. Surface syntax sugar (<T> syntax)
7. Reflection intrinsics

## Success Criteria
- comptime blocks evaluate at compile time
- Generic functions instantiate correctly
- Type reflection intrinsics work
- <T> syntax sugar works
- Spec coverage complete

## Notes
This replaces traditional generics+traits with a simpler model.
Trait resolution is essentially an interpreter anyway.

title: Spec: Define underscore identifier semantics precisely
Support 'return;' without expression for unit functions
close_reason: All return; tests pass - feature complete

title: Add ShlImm instruction to x86_64 MIR to avoid vreg allocation for constant shifts
Remove unused Jae instruction from x86_64 MIR or document its intended purpose
description: The x86_64 MIR has a Jae (jump if above or equal, unsigned) instruction added at mir.rs:307-308, but it's not used anywhere in the codebase. Either remove it if it was added speculatively, or document why it's there for future use (e.g., for unsigned comparisons in future features).

title: Add UI compiler tests for error message quality
description:
Spec tests verify conformance to the language specification (compile/fail, exit codes). They don't check error message content since that's implementation detail.

Add a separate UI test suite (like Rust's tests/ui/) that:
- Captures stderr output for compile-fail tests  
- Compares against .stderr golden files
- Verifies error messages are helpful and accurate
- Checks line/column numbers in diagnostics

This allows us to catch error message regressions without conflating them with spec conformance.

title: Add warning suppression mechanism (allow attribute)
description: Add ability to suppress specific warnings using attributes like #[allow(unreachable_code)] or #[allow(unused_variables)]. This would let users intentionally silence warnings in cases where they know the code is correct but triggers false positives, or when writing example/test code where certain warnings are expected.

title: Intern labels in x86_64 lowering instead of allocating Strings
Add match expressions to Rue
description: Add match expressions as a generalization of if/else. This is the foundation for later enum support. Minimal viable version: match on integer/bool values with literal patterns, wildcard pattern (_), and exhaustiveness checking for booleans.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 742f2a2c

title: else_args not copied in Branch terminator (x86_64)
description: In crates/rue-codegen/src/x86_64/cfg_lower.rs, the Branch terminator handling copies then_args to the then_block's params, but else_args are never copied to else_block's params. The comment says 'else_args are handled when we emit the else block's label' but this doesn't actually happen. Currently doesn't cause issues because short-circuit operators (&&/||) only pass booleans, but would fail for struct values passed via else_args.

title: Improve unused variable warning to include line number or scope context when multiple variables share the same name
Add equality operator support for structs and unit type
Spec: Add terminology section defining coercion, compatible type, panic, value
Implement ownership modes (Phase 1)
description:
Implement explicit ownership mode declarations for types: value, move, linear, rc.

## ADR Reference
See docs/designs/adr-014-ownership-modes.md

## Key Features
- value struct: deep copy on assignment, no aliasing
- move struct: ownership transfer, use-after-move detection
- linear struct: must be explicitly consumed (cannot be dropped)
- rc struct: reference counted, shared ownership

## Implementation Steps
1. Parser + AST for ownership mode keywords
2. RIR + Type representation with mode
3. Move semantics (VarState tracking, use-after-move errors)
4. Linear checking (must consume at scope exit)
5. Reference counting runtime support
6. Field compatibility rules
7. Specification and tests

## Success Criteria
- All four modes parse and type-check
- Use-after-move detected for move types
- Linear values must be consumed (compiler error otherwise)
- Reference counting works for rc types
- Spec coverage complete

## Notes
This is the foundation for memory safety. All other type system
features build on top of ownership modes.

title: Spec: Add enum variant patterns to grammar appendix
Add compile-time detection of negation on unsigned integer types
description: Currently, code like `let x: u8 = -5;` compiles successfully and panics at runtime with 'integer overflow'. This could be caught at compile time since negation of unsigned types will always overflow (except for 0). The compiler should emit a compile-time error when a negative literal is assigned to an unsigned type.

title: Enable aarch64-linux target after testing ELF linker with AArch64 relocations
Spec: Clarify negative array index behavior with signed types
Add side-effect tests to verify evaluation order when mutable state is implemented
description:
The current evaluation order tests in crates/rue-spec/cases/expressions/evaluation-order.toml use pure expressions (literals) that don't actually verify evaluation order. For example:

```rue
fn main() -> i32 { 1 + 2 }  // Would pass regardless of evaluation order
```

When mutable state (`mut`) is implemented, add tests with side effects:

```rue
fn main() -> i32 {
    let mut counter = 0;
    fn bump() -> i32 { counter = counter + 1; counter }
    bump() - bump()  // Should be 1 - 2 = -1 if left-to-right
}
```

This would definitively verify left-to-right evaluation order for binary operators, function arguments, etc.

Blocked on: mutable variable support

close_reason: Completed: Full aarch64 code generation backend with all 659 spec tests passing

close_reason: Completed: aarch64 MIR definitions implemented in mir.rs

title: Lower CFG to aarch64 MIR
close_reason: Completed: CFG lowering to aarch64 MIR implemented in cfg_lower.rs

title: Implement aarch64 register allocation
close_reason: Completed: aarch64 register allocation implemented in regalloc.rs with liveness analysis

title: Spec: Clarify integer type inference rules for complex expressions
Spec: Document absence of bitwise operators (or add them)
close_reason: Implemented bitwise operators (&, |, ^, ~, <<, >>)

title: Reserve type names as keywords
close_reason: Implemented: type names (i8, i16, i32, i64, u8, u16, u32, u64, bool) are now reserved keywords

title: Implement mutable value semantics with inout (Phase 2)
description:
Implement mutable value semantics with inout parameters and exclusivity checking.

## ADR Reference
See docs/designs/adr-015-mutable-value-semantics.md

## Key Features
- inout parameter passing mode for exclusive mutable access
- Explicit inout at call sites: foo(inout x)
- Law of exclusivity (no aliased mutation)
- Projection tracking (inout x.field)
- No stored references (inout is purely a calling convention)
- inout self for mutating methods

## Implementation Steps
1. Parser + AST for parameter modes (Default/Inout/Move)
2. Call site argument modes
3. RIR + AIR representation
4. Exclusivity analysis (path overlap detection)
5. Projection tracking for fields
6. Codegen (pass by pointer)
7. Method syntax with inout self

## Success Criteria
- inout parameters work correctly
- Exclusivity violations detected at compile time
- Field projections work (disjoint access allowed)
- Spec coverage complete

## Notes
This provides Rust-like safety without lifetime annotations.
The key insight: inout bindings cannot be stored, only passed.

title: Improve error spans for pattern type mismatches in match
Add comment documenting peek_type LHS-first behavior for arithmetic
Remove dead code in struct init analysis after field order check was added
close_reason: Removed dead code: HashMap allocation and lookups replaced with direct iteration, redundant extra fields check removed

title: Panic in CfgBuilder when return statement has unreachable code after it
description:
CfgBuilder panics with 'called `Option::unwrap()` on a `None` value' at build.rs:835 when a return statement is followed by unreachable code.

Reproduction:
```rue
fn main() -> i32 { return 0; let x = 1; 0 }
```

Expected: Should emit an 'unreachable code' warning and compile successfully.
Actual: Panics during CFG construction.

The unreachable code detection works correctly for break/continue, but not for return statements.
close_reason: Fixed: Check if return value expression diverged and propagate divergence instead of panicking. Added spec tests.

title: Spec: Clarify when shadowing takes effect in let statements
Add loop {} construct that produces never type
close_reason: Implemented loop {} expression with Never type semantics. All 732 tests pass.

title: Support multi-field struct returns from branch expressions
description: When returning a struct with multiple fields from a branch expression (if/else), only the first field is properly returned. This is because the Branch lowering doesn't propagate all field vregs through both branches. Single-field structs work correctly. Discovered while implementing tree1-sai.
close_reason: Fixed: Multi-field struct returns from branch expressions now work correctly. Added field vreg propagation through block parameters in both x86_64 and aarch64 backends. All 737 tests pass.

title: Add debug assertion in array_length fallback to catch invalid array type IDs
Support negative literal patterns in match expressions
description:
The pattern parser currently only accepts positive integer literals, boolean literals, and wildcards. Negative patterns like `-1 => ...` fail with a parse error:

```rue
match -1 {
    -1 => 42,  // error: expected 'Underscore' or something else or 'RBrace', found '-'
    _ => 0,
}
```

Supporting negative patterns would require either:
1. Parsing negation as part of the integer pattern (unary minus + literal)
2. Or storing signed literals directly in the lexer

For now this is a known limitation - users can work around it by using variables or guards when those are added.

title: Add CFG-level diagnostic infrastructure
description:
Add infrastructure for CFG analysis passes to emit warnings.

Currently only Sema produces warnings (via SemaOutput.warnings). For flow-sensitive diagnostics like dead code detection, we need CFG analysis passes to also contribute warnings.

Changes needed:
- Decide where CFG analysis runs (new phase after CFG construction, or as part of CfgBuilder)
- Add mechanism for CFG passes to collect warnings with source spans
- Integrate CFG warnings into CompileState.warnings
- Consider: should CfgBuilder track dead code spans when it skips unreachable statements?

Design considerations:
- Keep it simple - don't over-engineer for hypothetical future passes
- Warnings need Span for good error messages
- May want to make warnings configurable (allow disabling specific warnings)
close_reason: Infrastructure already implemented: CfgBuilder collects warnings, CfgOutput.warnings propagates them to CompileState, unreachable code detection works with 25 passing spec tests.

title: Spec: Specify evaluation order for binary operator operands
close_reason: Implemented unified evaluation order specification in section 4.0 of the Expressions chapter

title: Unsigned comparison operators use signed comparison instructions
description:
## Problem

The codegen for comparison operators (`<`, `>`, `<=`, `>=`) always emits signed comparison instructions regardless of the operand types:

- x86-64: Uses `Setl`/`Setg`/`Setle`/`Setge` (signed) instead of `Setb`/`Seta`/`Setbe`/`Setae` (unsigned)
- aarch64: Uses `Cond::Lt`/`Cond::Gt`/`Cond::Le`/`Cond::Ge` (signed) instead of `Cond::Lo`/`Cond::Hi`/`Cond::Ls`/`Cond::Hs` (unsigned)

## Reproduction

```rue
fn main() -> i32 {
    let x: u32 = 1;
    let y: u32 = 4294967295;  // 0xFFFFFFFF, which is -1 in signed
    if x > y { 1 } else { 0 }  // Returns 1 (wrong!) because signed 1 > -1
}
```

Expected: Returns 0 (unsigned 1 is NOT greater than 4294967295)
Actual: Returns 1 (signed comparison treats y as -1)

## Fix

The type information needs to be threaded from sema through codegen. The comparison instruction selection in `cfg_lower.rs` needs to check if the operand type is signed or unsigned and emit the appropriate instruction variant.

Affected files:
- `crates/rue-codegen/src/x86_64/cfg_lower.rs`
- `crates/rue-codegen/src/aarch64/cfg_lower.rs`

The `CfgInstData::Lt`/`Gt`/`Le`/`Ge` variants may need to carry signedness information, or the lowering needs to look up the type from the instruction.
close_reason: Fixed: Updated codegen for both x86_64 and aarch64 to use unsigned comparison instructions (setb/seta/setbe/setae on x86 and Lo/Hi/Ls/Hs on aarch64) when comparing unsigned integer types. Added is_unsigned() helper to Type enum and comprehensive spec tests.

title: we have both docs/langauge.md and rue-spec/spec.md, we should consolidate and make sure that everything refers to the right place.
close_reason: Consolidated to docs/language.md - deleted rue-spec/spec.md as it was outdated and redundant. All references already pointed to docs/language.md.

title: Redesign spec structure with paragraph references and test traceability
close_reason: Implemented spec structure with paragraph references, test traceability, and coverage checking

title: Add else if syntax support in parser
close_reason: Implemented else if syntax in parser

title: Spec: Clarify intrinsic expression placement in grammar
Add compile-time range validation for integer literals
description: Literals that fit in u64 but exceed the target type's range (e.g., `let x: i32 = 9999999999;`) are currently accepted without error and silently truncated at codegen. The compiler should emit a compile-time error when a literal value exceeds the range of its target type. This affects all integer types.

title: Extend compile-time bounds checking to handle constant-foldable index expressions
description:
The try_get_const_index function in sema.rs only handles direct integer literals and negated literals. More complex constant expressions like arr[1 + 1] or arr[CONST] won't get compile-time bounds checking. Consider extending constant evaluation to handle:
- Binary operations on constants (1 + 1, 2 * 3)
- References to const variables
- Parenthesized expressions

This would catch more errors at compile time rather than runtime.

title: Add target field to spec test runner for target-specific compile-fail tests
Add bounds checking for array indexing
close_reason: Implemented compile-time and runtime bounds checking for array indexing

title: Lift CFG construction out of codegen into compile pipeline
description:
Currently both x86_64 and aarch64 backends independently build CFG from AIR inside their generate() functions. This is:

1. Wasteful - CFG is architecture-independent, built twice
2. A layering violation - CFG should be built before backend selection
3. Blocking for diagnostics - flow analysis needs CFG but shouldn't depend on codegen

Changes needed:
- Move CfgBuilder::build() call from rue-codegen backends to rue-compiler
- Add cfgs: Vec<Cfg> (or similar) to CompileState
- Update x86_64::generate() and aarch64::generate() to accept &Cfg instead of &Air
- Remove rue-cfg dependency from individual backends (keep in rue-codegen for re-export if needed)

This enables CFG to be built once and reused for both analysis passes and all backends.
close_reason: CFG construction lifted to compile_to_air in rue-compiler. Both x86_64 and aarch64 backends now accept &Cfg instead of &Air. All 659 tests pass.

title: Callee-saved registers not restored on x86_64 returns
description:
Prologue pushes any used callee-saved regs, but emit_epilogue only does mov rsp, rbp; pop rbp, leaving saved registers on the stack and clobbered in the caller (ABI violation and possible crashes).

Location: cfg_lower.rs lines 126-134 (epilogue) and call sites around lines 1336-1385.

Fix: Epilogue needs to pop saved callee regs in reverse order before restoring rbp.
close_reason: Already fixed in commit 1b533d7 (Dec 17, 2025): Fixed epilogue to use lea rsp,[rbp-N] before popping callee-saved registers. All 866 tests pass confirming the fix.

title: Add note/hint system for compiler warnings
description: Add ability for compiler warnings and errors to include additional context notes or hints. For example, unreachable code warnings could include 'note: any code following this expression is unreachable' similar to Rust's diagnostic style. This would improve compiler UX by providing more context about why the warning was raised.

title: Spec: Add implementation limits section (max array size, params, etc)
Spec: State lexer uses maximal munch (or specify alternative)
Add UI test for deeply nested variable shadowing (3+ levels)
Spec: Specify struct memory layout (padding, alignment, size)
allows dead code to exist
description:
things like break and continue can introduce dead code, we should warn on this

Superseded by:
- tree1-fwv: Lift CFG construction out of codegen
- tree1-ci1: Add CFG-level diagnostic infrastructure  
- tree1-mcu: Implement dead code warning

See those issues for the detailed implementation plan.
close_reason: Superseded by tree1-fwv, tree1-ci1, tree1-mcu - broken down into proper refactoring steps

title: Spec: Standardize normative language (shall/may/must hierarchy)
Spec: Formally define match exhaustiveness checking
close_reason: Formally defined match exhaustiveness checking in spec. Added irrefutable/refutable pattern definitions (4.7:3-7), exhaustiveness definition (4.7:8), requirement (4.7:9), and algorithm rules (4.7:10). Updated all spec test references.

title: Implement dead code warning
close_reason: Dead code warning is fully implemented and all 11 unreachable code tests pass

title: aarch64 runtime support
close_reason: Completed: aarch64 runtime support with Mach-O linking, all tests pass on macOS

title: Avoid unnecessary tokens.clone() in handle_emit when only emitting AST
description: In handle_emit(), tokens are cloned when creating the parser even when we only need the AST and won't use the tokens vector again. This is a minor inefficiency in a debugging tool.
close_reason: Already fixed - no tokens.clone() in codebase

title: Spec: Clarify loop expression type when break is present
Add tests for nested arrays, arrays in structs, and arrays as function params
Add comprehensive IR and assembly dump flags
close_reason: Implemented unified --emit flag with stages: tokens, ast, rir, air, cfg, mir, asm

title: Emit unused variable warning for shadowed variables that are never used
description: In check_unused_locals_in_current_scope() (sema.rs:249-253), we skip checking variables that shadow outer scope variables (old_value.is_some()). This means if you shadow a variable and never use the shadowing binding, you won't get a warning. Example: let x = 1; { let x = 2; } // no warning even though inner x is unused. Consider emitting a warning for shadowed variables that are never read.
close_reason: Implemented: shadowed variables now emit unused warnings. Added 3 UI tests.

title: Integer literal type inference is left-to-right only
description:
Integer literals default to i32 in infer_type(). Combined with left-to-right type inference for comparisons, this means:

```rue
let x: i64 = 100;
if x == 100 { ... }   // OK: 100 checked against i64
if 100 == x { ... }   // FAIL: 100 infers as i32, x is i64 -> type mismatch
```

Options to consider:
1. Document this as intentional (left-to-right inference)
2. Add integer literal type suffixes (`100i64`, `42u8`, etc.)
3. Implement bidirectional propagation where unsuffixed literals can adopt type from context on either side

This is a design decision more than a bug. Rust uses suffixes and bidirectional inference for literals.

title: Unify x86_64 and aarch64 lowering paths - both should go through CFG
close_reason: Deleted unused x86_64/lower.rs (1700+ lines of dead code). Both x86_64 and aarch64 backends now consistently use only cfg_lower.rs for the CFG → MIR lowering path. All 732 tests pass.

title: aarch64-macos target and runtime support
close_reason: Implemented aarch64-macos target and runtime support. Runtime compiles and tests pass. Note: Codegen backend for aarch64 is separate work tracked in tree1-55h.

title: Add Aarch64Macos target to rue-target
close_reason: Added Aarch64Macos to Target enum with all necessary methods

title: Implement aarch64_macos runtime module
close_reason: Created aarch64_macos.rs with macOS syscall implementations

title: Update lib.rs to use aarch64_macos module
close_reason: Updated lib.rs with macOS platform support and conditional compilation

title: LabelInterner should deduplicate labels
description:
The LabelInterner in rue-codegen/src/x86_64/mir.rs does not deduplicate labels. Calling intern() with the same label string twice creates two separate Symbol entries.

This is a key property of interning - the same string should return the same symbol. Benefits:
1. Memory savings from not storing duplicate strings
2. O(1) equality comparison via symbol ID
3. Consistent semantics with rue-intern's Interner

Current behavior:
```rust
let mut interner = LabelInterner::new();
let s1 = interner.intern(".Lmain_0");
let s2 = interner.intern(".Lmain_0");
assert_ne!(s1, s2); // Currently passes - they are different symbols!
```

Expected behavior:
```rust
let mut interner = LabelInterner::new();
let s1 = interner.intern(".Lmain_0");
let s2 = interner.intern(".Lmain_0");
assert_eq!(s1, s2); // Should return the same symbol
```

Implementation options:
1. Add a HashMap<&str, Symbol> for lookups (like rue-intern does)
2. Use rue-intern's Interner directly instead of a custom one

Note: The current code happens to work because each label is only interned once (labels are generated uniquely via label_counter). But this violates the principle of interning and could cause subtle bugs if code changes.
close_reason: Implemented: Ported LabelId pattern from x86_64 to aarch64 backend. Labels are now u32 indices instead of heap-allocated strings.

title: Implement generators and iteration (Phase 4)
description:
Implement generator functions with yield syntax that compile to state machines.

## ADR Reference
See docs/designs/adr-017-generators.md

## Key Features
- fn* (or gen fn) syntax for generator functions
- yield expression for suspending and returning values
- State machine transformation at compile time
- Generator<T> type with next() method
- For loop integration with iterator protocol
- Bidirectional generators (send/receive)
- Proper ownership handling for captured values

## Implementation Steps
1. Parser + AST changes for fn* and yield
2. Generator analysis (collect locals, find yield points)
3. State machine transformation
4. Generator type definition
5. For loop desugaring
6. Ownership integration (linear captures = linear generator)

## Success Criteria
- fn* generators compile and work
- yield suspends and resumes correctly
- For loops iterate over generators
- Ownership interacts correctly
- Spec coverage complete

title: Detect duplicate struct field names in sema
close_reason: Implemented duplicate struct field detection in sema.rs. Added DuplicateField error kind and spec test.

title: Spec paragraph 4.0:1 should be marked normative or have test coverage
description:
The first paragraph in docs/spec/src/04-expressions/README.md:

```
r[4.0.1]
An expression is a syntactic construct that evaluates to a value. Every expression has a type.
```

This paragraph lacks the `#normative` tag, so it doesn't require test coverage. However, the statement 'Every expression has a type' is a normative requirement.

Options:
1. Mark it as `r[4.0.1#normative]` and add a test
2. Split into two paragraphs: informative intro + normative type requirement
3. Leave as informative if the typing requirement is covered elsewhere

title: Spec: Clarify comparison operator chaining behavior
Lexer cannot parse integer literals larger than i64::MAX
description: Integer literals larger than i64::MAX (9223372036854775807) fail to parse with 'unexpected character' error. For example, `let x: u64 = 9223372036854775808;` fails even though this value is valid for u64 (it's exactly 2^63). The lexer appears to be internally using i64 to store literal values, which causes overflow for larger values. This affects u64 literals in the range [i64::MAX + 1, u64::MAX].

title: Spec: Clarify parsing/semantics of negative minimum signed literals
Optimize --emit to avoid double-parsing when emitting both AST and later stages
description: When using --emit ast along with --emit rir/air/cfg/mir/asm, the code currently parses the source twice: once explicitly for AST output, and again inside compile_frontend(). Consider refactoring compile_frontend to accept an already-parsed AST, or extract the AST from the frontend state instead of parsing separately.
close_reason: Refactored --emit to use single-pass compilation. Each stage (tokenize, parse, semantic analysis) now runs at most once. Added compile_frontend_from_ast() that accepts an already-parsed AST, and the AST is now stored in CompileState for emission.

title: Use scope stack instead of HashMap::clone() for locals in sema loop analysis
Support unit-returning functions (fn foo() { ... })
close_reason: Implemented unit-returning functions: both implicit (fn foo() { }) and explicit (fn foo() -> () { }) syntax now work

title: Spec: Define coercion formally, especially for never type
Bidirectional type inference for arithmetic operators
close_reason: Fixed: infer_type now recursively infers arithmetic operand types instead of hardcoding i32

title: Add array type support to Rue
close_reason: Implemented: Added fixed-size arrays with Rust-like syntax [T; N], array literals, element indexing, and mutation. All 25 spec tests pass.

title: Spec: Define behavior for unreachable match patterns after wildcard
close_reason: Added spec paragraphs 4.7.17-4.7.22 defining unreachable pattern behavior, plus 6 spec tests

title: Add warning for duplicate patterns in match expressions
description:
The compiler currently accepts duplicate patterns in match expressions without any warning:

```rue
match 1 {
    1 => 10,
    1 => 20,  // No warning, silently unreachable
    _ => 0,
}
```

Should emit a warning like 'unreachable pattern' for patterns that can never match because an earlier pattern already covers them.
close_reason: Implemented unreachable pattern warnings for match expressions. Detects: duplicate integer patterns, duplicate boolean patterns, patterns after wildcard, and duplicate wildcards. Added span information to RirPattern for accurate diagnostics. Added 10 UI tests covering all cases.

title: Spec: Consider adding else-if as first-class syntax
Redesign spec system with traceability and publishable format
description:
Implement a specification system inspired by Ferrocene/FLS, C/C++ ISO standards, and the Rust Reference. Goals:

1. **Traceability**: Link each test to a specific spec paragraph ID (e.g., 3.2.1:5)
2. **Hierarchical organization**: Chapter > Section > Subsection > Paragraph structure
3. **Rule categories**: Syntax, Legality Rules, Dynamic Semantics, Undefined Behavior
4. **Normative vs Informative**: Mark which content is binding vs explanatory
5. **Publishable format**: Use format convertible to HTML for website publication
6. **Test-spec bidirectional linking**: Each spec paragraph can list its covering tests

This involves:
- Designing new spec document format (likely RST or extended Markdown)
- Redesigning test TOML structure with spec paragraph references
- Migrating all 855 existing tests to new format
- Building tooling to generate traceability matrix
- Updating spec runner to support new format
close_reason: Completed spec system redesign with Ferrocene/FLS-style traceability

title: Add --target flag to CLI
description: Add --target flag to CLI argument parsing. Support 'x86-64-linux' and 'aarch64-linux' values. Default to host detection via Target::host(). Thread target through to compile() call.
close_reason: Added --target flag to CLI. Parses x86-64-linux and aarch64-linux values, defaults to Target::host(). Target is stored in Options struct and displayed in compile success message. Full testing requires Linux (runtime dependency).

title: Thread target through compiler pipeline
description: Update rue-compiler to accept Target parameter. Add CompileOptions struct. Dispatch to correct codegen backend based on target.arch(). Currently only x86_64 implemented, but structure allows adding more.
close_reason: Added CompileOptions struct with target field. Created compile_with_options() that dispatches to architecture-specific backends. compile() now calls compile_with_options with defaults. CLI updated to pass target through. aarch64 returns clear 'not yet implemented' error.

title: Parameterize linker by target
description: Remove hardcoded x86-64 constants from rue-linker. Use target.elf_machine() instead of 0x3E. Use target.default_base_addr() instead of 0x400000. Use target.page_size() instead of 0x1000. Update both emit.rs (object files) and linker.rs (executables).
close_reason: Completed: Linker and ObjectBuilder now parameterized by Target

title: Add --linker flag with system linker support
description: Add --linker flag to CLI. Support 'internal' (default) and system linkers like 'clang', 'ld'. When using system linker: write object file to temp location, invoke external linker command, clean up temp file. This provides fallback when internal linker has issues.
close_reason: Completed: Added --linker flag with support for internal and system linkers

title: Migrate website from SCSS to Tailwind CSS with light/dark mode
close_reason: Implemented Tailwind CSS migration with light/dark mode support

title: Consider preallocating scope_stack inner Vec with small capacity
description: In AnalysisContext::push_scope() (sema.rs:94), the inner Vec is created with default capacity. Since most scopes have only a few variables, preallocating with a small capacity (4-8) could reduce allocations. Minor optimization.
close_reason: Already fixed - push_scope now preallocates with capacity 2

title: Implement struct field initialization order checking
close_reason: Implemented struct field initialization order checking

title: Spec: Define empty struct semantics (size, construction)
Spec: Specify struct field initializer evaluation order
Add enum types (discriminated unions without data)
description: Implement basic enum types (discriminated unions without associated data). See docs/designs/adr-013-enum-types.md for the full design.
close_reason: Implemented basic enum types with parsing, type checking, and codegen

title: Array parameters to functions are not passed correctly
Extract shared codegen utilities between x86_64 and aarch64 backends
description:
Several functions are duplicated verbatim between the x86_64 and aarch64 backends:
- array_type_def
- type_slot_count
- array_element_slot_count
- struct_field_slot_offset
- collect_array_scalar_vregs
- collect_struct_scalar_vregs

Consider extracting these to a shared utility module to reduce code duplication and maintenance burden. Low priority refactoring.

title: Update comment in sema.rs to remove stale 'spec 4.6:5' reference
Make return; equivalent to return () in spec and implementation
close_reason: Implemented: return; is now equivalent to return () in both spec and implementation

title: Add test for 3-level nested arrays
description: Current nested array tests only cover 2 levels of nesting. Add tests for 3-level nested arrays like `[[[i32; 2]; 2]; 2]` to ensure the recursive flattening in collect_array_scalar_vregs works correctly for deeper nesting.

title: Unsigned integer overflow checking
description: Add overflow checking for unsigned integer types (u8, u16, u32, u64) and remaining signed types (i8, i16, i64). Currently only i32 has overflow checking - other types wrap silently. The spec says overflow should be undefined behavior (panic with exit code 101).
close_reason: Implemented overflow checking for all integer types (u8, u16, u32, u64, i8, i16, i64) on both x86_64 and aarch64. Signed types use V (overflow) flag, unsigned types use C (carry) flag for native-width and range checking for sub-word types.

title: Optimize sub-word overflow checks to avoid extra MovRR
description:
In x86_64/cfg_lower.rs emit_overflow_check(), for i8/i16 overflow checking, the code does:
1. MovRR to copy result to sext_vreg
2. Movsx8To64/Movsx16To64 to sign-extend
3. CmpRR to compare

This allocates an extra vreg and emits a copy. A more efficient approach would sign-extend directly into a new vreg without the intermediate MovRR, saving one instruction per overflow check for sub-word signed types.

Location: crates/rue-codegen/src/x86_64/cfg_lower.rs:1276-1304

title: Extract common sub-word range checking code in AArch64 cfg_lower
description:
The emit_overflow_check_add, emit_overflow_check_sub, emit_overflow_check_mul, and emit_overflow_check_neg methods in aarch64/cfg_lower.rs share significant duplicated code for sub-word type range checking (U8, U16, I8, I16).

The pattern that repeats in each method:
- U8: CmpImm with 255, BCond Ls
- U16: MovImm 65535, CmpRR, BCond Ls  
- I8: Sxtb, CmpRR, BCond Eq
- I16: Sxth, CmpRR, BCond Eq

Consider extracting a emit_subword_range_check(ty, result_vreg, ok_label) helper to reduce duplication.

Location: crates/rue-codegen/src/aarch64/cfg_lower.rs:1113-1576

title: Implement UI tests for compiler features (warnings, diagnostics)
close_reason: Implemented UI tests infrastructure with rue-test-runner shared crate and rue-ui-tests crate. Added initial tests for unreachable code and unused variable warnings.

title: Type check if-expressions without else branches require unit type
close_reason: Implemented: if-expressions without else branches now require their then branch to have unit type. Added Expr::Unit to AST, InstData::UnitConst to RIR, AirInstData::UnitConst to AIR. All 903 tests pass.

title: Add test for outer index bounds check on nested arrays
description: Verify that out-of-bounds access on the outer index of a nested array triggers a runtime panic. For example, `arr[10][0]` on a `[[i32; 2]; 2]` should panic. This tests that bounds checking works correctly for chained indexing where the outer IndexGet is used as a base for the inner IndexGet.

title: Support else if chains in parser
Preallocate scope_stack inner Vec with small capacity
close_reason: Implemented: Changed Vec::new() to Vec::with_capacity(2) in push_scope() at sema.rs:94. Capacity of 2 was chosen because most scopes (loop bodies, if/match arms) have 0-2 variables; function bodies have more but are less frequent. This reduces allocations for the common case.

title: Remove unused serde dependency from rue-spec/BUCK
Add logos and chumsky dependencies
description: Add logos and chumsky to third-party/Cargo.toml, run reindeer buckify, and update BUCK files for rue-lexer and rue-parser.
close_reason: Added logos and chumsky dependencies to third-party/Cargo.toml, vendored them, created fixups, and updated rue-lexer/BUCK and rue-parser/BUCK to include the new dependencies. Build succeeds.

title: Support nested struct field mutation
description: Allow mutating fields of nested structs, e.g. `a.b.x = 5`. Currently only single-level field mutation is supported (`p.x = 5`). Nested field access works for reading but not for assignment.

title: Port parser to chumsky
description: Create new chumsky-based parser in rue-parser/src/chumsky_parser.rs. Implement function_parser, block_parser, statement_parser, expr_parser with Pratt parsing, if_parser, while_parser, and atom_parser.
close_reason: Successfully ported parser to chumsky with Pratt parsing. All 27 parser tests pass (4 chumsky + 23 original). All 453 spec tests pass.

title: Port frontend to logos/chumsky
description: Port the rue compiler's lexer from manual scanning to logos (derive-macro lexer) and parser from recursive descent to chumsky (parser combinators with Pratt parsing). This improves maintainability and leverages battle-tested parsing libraries.
acceptance_criteria:
- All existing spec tests pass unchanged
- Lexer uses logos derive macro for tokenization
- Parser uses chumsky combinators with Pratt parsing for expressions
- Error messages maintain span accuracy
- Old implementations removed after validation
close_reason:
Epic complete. All acceptance criteria met:
- All 453 spec tests pass
- Lexer uses logos derive macro (LogosLexer in logos_lexer.rs)
- Parser uses chumsky with Pratt parsing (ChumskyParser in chumsky_parser.rs)
- Error messages maintain span accuracy (updated golden tests)
- Old implementations removed (old parser.rs deleted, lib.rs simplified)

title: Remove old lexer/parser code
description: After validation, remove the old manual lexer and recursive descent parser implementations.
close_reason: Old lexer and parser code removed. rue-lexer now re-exports LogosLexer as Lexer, rue-parser re-exports ChumskyParser as Parser. Old parser.rs deleted. All 453 spec tests pass.

title: Support multi-field struct returns from branch expressions
description: When returning a struct with multiple fields from a branch expression (if/else), only the first field is properly returned. This is because the Branch lowering doesn't propagate all field vregs through both branches. Single-field structs work correctly. Discovered while implementing work-sai.
close_reason: Fixed: Multi-field struct returns from branch expressions now work correctly. Added field vreg propagation through block parameters in both x86_64 and aarch64 backends. All 737 tests pass.

title: Integration and error handling
description: Bridge chumsky SimpleSpan to rue_span::Span. Convert chumsky Rich errors to CompileError. Update rue-compiler to use new implementations.
close_reason: Integration complete: SimpleSpan to rue_span::Span bridging via to_rue_span(), Rich error to CompileError conversion via convert_error(). New implementations pass all tests alongside existing code.

title: no support for warnings, only errors
close_reason: Implemented warning support infrastructure including unused variable detection

title: Validation and switchover
description: Run all spec tests against new implementation. Create comparison tests between old and new. Switch rue-compiler to use new lexer/parser.
close_reason: Validation complete: all 35 parser tests pass (including while loop tests), all 26 lexer tests pass, all 453 spec tests pass. Fixed semicolon-optional control flow statements (if/while/break/continue) by implementing Rust-like BlockItem parsing.

title: allows dead code to exist
description:
things like break and continue can introduce dead code, we should warn on this

Superseded by:
- tree1-fwv: Lift CFG construction out of codegen
- tree1-ci1: Add CFG-level diagnostic infrastructure  
- tree1-mcu: Implement dead code warning

See those issues for the detailed implementation plan.
close_reason: Superseded by tree1-fwv, tree1-ci1, tree1-mcu - broken down into proper refactoring steps

title: Add struct support to Rue
description: Add user-defined struct types with value semantics as foundation for MVS and strings. Includes: struct declarations, struct literals, field access, field mutation (with mut bindings), copy semantics for passing.
close_reason: Completed struct support implementation with value semantics - 510 tests passing

title: Support structs as function parameters
description: Allow passing structs as function parameters with copy semantics. Currently struct types can be declared and used locally, but passing them to functions fails. Requires ABI work to properly copy struct fields when passing to functions.
close_reason:
Implemented struct parameter passing with copy semantics. Changes:
1. Updated sema to track ABI slots (flattened fields) instead of parameter count
2. Updated ParamInfo to store abi_slot offset for struct parameter field access
3. Updated FieldGet lowering to handle Param base (struct parameter field access)
4. Updated Call lowering to flatten struct arguments into their individual fields
5. Added struct_field_counts to Lower for determining field counts at codegen time
All 14 new struct parameter tests pass, along with all 524 existing tests.

title: Support structs as function return types
description: Allow returning structs from functions with copy semantics. Currently struct types can be declared and used locally, but returning them from functions fails. Requires ABI work to properly return struct values.
close_reason: Implemented struct return types. Added 16 spec tests covering single-field, multi-field, chained, nested calls, conditionals, and recursive struct returns. Modified the codegen to pass struct definitions from Sema, handle struct returns in the Ret instruction (returning all fields via RAX, RDX, etc.), and handle struct returns in Call (receiving all fields from return registers). Note: Multi-field struct returns from branch expressions need additional work for full support.

title: no divergent type
description: we have no ! type, which means break/continue are statements rather than expressions, this is less than ideal
design:
## Design Summary

Add a `Never` type (`!`) to Rue that represents computations that never produce a value (they diverge). This enables `break` and `continue` to be used as expressions.

### Key Changes

1. **Type System**: Add `Type::Never` variant that can coerce to any type
2. **Sema**: Update branch unification to handle never type subsumption
3. **Break/Continue**: Change from `Unit` type to `Never` type

### Implementation Phases

**Phase 1 (this issue):**
- Add `Never` type variant
- Update break/continue to return `Never`
- Update branch unification
- Comprehensive tests

**Phase 2 (future):**
- `loop { }` keyword
- `-> !` return type annotation
- Dead code detection

See `docs/designs/adr-010-never-type.md` for full design.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All tests in `crates/rue-spec/cases/14-never-type.toml` pass
- [ ] Existing tests continue to pass (`./test.sh`)
- [ ] `break` and `continue` can appear in expression position inside loops
- [ ] Type inference correctly uses the non-diverging branch type
- [ ] Error messages for type mismatches are still clear
- [ ] `break`/`continue` outside loops still produce clear errors
close_reason:
Implemented Never type (!) for break/continue expressions. Changes:
- Added Type::Never variant to rue-air/src/types.rs
- Updated break/continue to return Never type in rue-air/src/sema.rs
- Updated branch unification to handle Never type coercion
- Updated infer_type to use non-diverging branch for type inference
- Updated codegen to handle divergent branches (rue-codegen/src/x86_64/lower.rs)
- Added 29 comprehensive test cases in crates/rue-spec/cases/14-never-type.toml

All 646 spec tests pass.

title: Port lexer to logos
description: Create new logos-based lexer in rue-lexer/src/logos_lexer.rs. Implement TokenKind with logos derive macro, wrapper function to maintain API, and EOF handling.
close_reason: Created logos-based lexer in logos_lexer.rs with LogosLexer struct. All tests pass (20 total). The new lexer maintains API compatibility and produces identical output to the original lexer.
